_description: RustPython protocol traits. An `impl <Trait> for <Type>` whose Trait is one of these implements a Python protocol SLOT (repr / hash / subscript / iter / call / ...). Its methods carry NO `#[pymethod]` attribute but are Python-reachable — this is the `#[pyclass(with(Trait))]` surface. The internals-mapper attributes such impls to the `protocol` reachability tier; the panic-site auditor treats panics inside them as Python-reachable. Seeded from the fuzzing seed tool's PROTOCOL_TRAITS list (rustpython-findings/tools/unwrap_scan/src/main.rs:48-70) and verified against crates/vm/src/types/ slot traits.
_note: Matched by the LAST path segment, so `crate::protocol::Representable` matches `Representable`. Kept as a flat list because RustPython's slot traits live in one module (types/) and are referenced unqualified at impl sites.

trait_to_python_slot._note: Best-effort mapping of a protocol trait to the Python dunder(s)/protocol it backs, for report readability. Not exhaustive; a trait may back several slots.
trait_to_python_slot.Unconstructible: types that reject construction from Python
